evals.prompt: Create a module called `featured_resources` for a Drupal 10 site that renders a themed list of featured learning resources. Implement hook_theme() with a template that displays resource items with titles and links. Attach a CSS library for styling the list. Use render arrays with #theme, NOT raw HTML strings. Use proper Drupal template naming conventions. We want D11 compatibility.
evals.expected_output: A theming module with hook_theme(), a hyphen-named Twig template, render arrays using #theme, and an attached CSS library via .libraries.yml
expectations: hook_theme() declares ALL template variables with default values in the 'variables' key (not leaving variables undeclared)

Template filename uses hyphens: e.g., featured-resources-list.html.twig (NOT underscores like featured_resources_list.html.twig) -- Drupal convention is underscores in hook name map to hyphens in filename
Render array uses #theme key referencing the hook_theme entry name (NOT building raw HTML strings with Markup::create())
CSS/JS attached via #attached['library'] array referencing featured_resources/{library-name} (NOT inline <script> or <style> tags, NOT drupal_add_css/drupal_add_js)
featured_resources.libraries.yml file exists defining the CSS library with relative path and optional dependencies
Controller or page callback returns a render array (NOT a plain string or Response object for themed content)
Template file exists in templates/ subdirectory of the module (correct location for module templates)
The module enables successfully: ddev drush en featured_resources returns exit code 0
